# BI-260010: Establish Reusable UI Component Library and Vertical Slice Structure

- Backlog Item ID: BI-260010
- Title: Establish reusable UI component library and vertical slice structure
- Status: Done (reopened and closed after code review fixes: 2026-06-10)
- Persona/Actor: Developer/owner adding features to the public site
- Value statement: For the developer, deliver composable UI component templates and a
  vertical-slice folder convention to achieve faster, consistent feature delivery with zero
  duplicated styles or markup.
- Context: The site has grown to the point where CSS duplication across three legal pages and the
  absence of any reusable UI baseline creates friction when adding or changing features.
- Problem statement: Adding a new page requires copy-pasting layout patterns, card markup, and
  form field structure from existing pages. Three policy pages share identical CSS that must be
  updated in three places. There is no established folder convention for feature-scoped code.
- Expected outcome: A developer building a new page imports Button, Card, FormField, or CheckList
  components; policy-style pages use PolicyArticle; feature code lives under `src/features/<name>/`.
- Success metric(s):
  - Zero duplicated policy CSS blocks in page files after the change.
  - At least four UI component templates in `src/components/ui/` consumed in practice.
  - `src/features/` vertical-slice convention documented and scaffolded.
  - All 52 unit tests and 38 e2e tests pass after the change.
- Acceptance criteria (behavior):
  - `/privacy`, `/terms`, `/google-addon/support` render identically to before.
  - `/google-addon` check-list still shows green ✓ bullets (now via CheckList component).
  - Sign-in form on `/` and `/sign-in` still functions end-to-end.
  - A style change to `.policy h2` in `global.css` reflects on all three legal pages without
    editing individual page files.
- Non-goals: Full form control library (radio, select, checkbox, textarea); index.astro
  decomposition into feature slices; any backend or route changes.
- Constraints: No routing or public link changes; file size ≤ 200 lines per new component;
  each implementation step must be independently releasable.
- Dependencies: None.
- Risks/assumptions: Risk is low — changes are additive (new files) plus safe CSS moves
  (identical rules across pages, no scoping needed). The only change to an existing file's
  behavior is `SignInForm.astro` consuming FormField; its external props interface is unchanged.
- Validation approach: `npm test` + `npx playwright test` + visual spot-check of legal pages,
  add-on page check-list, and sign-in form.
- Priority score: User value=3, Urgency=2, Risk reduction=4, Effort=3 → (3+2+4)/3 = 3.0
- Target window: Current sprint
- Links:
  - Requirement entry: REQ-QR-260010.md
  - ADR: ADR-260010.md
  - ICR: n/a
  - PR/changeset: n/a
